summary: Matched a batch of previously stubbed or placeholder-typed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c, mostly game-mode minor-scene setup helpers and StartMeleeData/VsModeData initialization paths. The PR replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in gm_1B03.h with concrete void, MinorScene*, PlayerInitData*, and StartMeleeData* signatures, added a StartMeleeData* prototype for gm_80189CDC in gm_1832.h, and refined gmmain global struct layout in types.h by naming VsModeData fields at offsets 0x1490 and 0x1710.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801B1F70, a StartMeleeData setup path that copies rules and player data from gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_D10, resets rules/player arrays with gm_80167A64 and gm_80167A14, adjusts per-player flags, calls gm_801B0620/gm_801B0664, then runs gm_8016F088 and gm_80189CDC.
Changed fn_801B1F6C from an empty void(void) stub to void fn_801B1F6C(int unused), matching its use as data->rules.x3C callback.

Implemented small OnLoad/init helpers gm_801B6808_OnLoad, gm_801B6834, and gm_801B685C that populate globals such as gm_804D68E8/gm_804D68E9/gm_804D68F0/gm_804D68F1 and call gm_801677F0, gm_80167B50, and lb_8001C550.

Implemented gm_801B69C0(StartMeleeData*) to initialize a rules block with many bitfields and constants, including xE = 0x11D, x2C = 0.5F, x20 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFBFCFF, callback x44 = gm_80182174, and several boolean rule flags.

Implemented many minor-scene wrapper functions in the 0x801B8BB4-0x801B9254 range, forwarding MinorScene* and specific VsModeData pointers from gmMainLib_804D3EE0 to shared helpers gm_801A5618, gm_801A5680, gm_801A5754, gm_801A57A8, gm_801A583C, gm_801A5AF0, gm_801A5C3C, gm_801A5EC8, gm_801A5F00, and gm_801A5F64.

Recovered PlayerInitData callback signatures for fn_801B8C5C, fn_801B8E50, and fn_801B9060; these callbacks directly write typed PlayerInitData fields such as x12, x20, x1C, x18, and xB instead of using M2C_FIELD.
Implemented gm_801BED14 as a simple scene/state transition helper calling gm_801A42E8(1) and gm_801A42D4().

Implemented gm_801BF4DC(MinorScene*) to initialize a StartMeleeData block and gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_1710 data for a mode-specific match setup, including rules from gm_801BF6B8/gm_801BF694 and player character/color/stocks/cpu fields from gm_801BF648/gm_801BF670.
Changed gm_801BF648 return type from u8 to s8, matching backing storage gm_8049E548.pad_0 and use as signed character kind data.
Updated gm_1832.h to include melee/gm/types.h and changed gm_80189CDC from unknown params to gm_80189CDC(StartMeleeData*).
Updated struct gmm_x0 in types.h by replacing padding at 0x1490 with VsModeData unk_1490, adding pad_15D0, and adding a VsModeData field named unk_1710 before x1850 GameRules.
smart_moves: Used shared gm_801A56xx/gm_801A57xx/gm_801A5xxx helper calls for large groups of minor-scene functions instead of duplicating setup logic, exposing a repeated wrapper pattern across multiple VsModeData slots.
Recovered concrete callback prototypes for PlayerInitData modifiers, which allowed replacing raw M2C_FIELD offset writes with typed field assignments.
Moved function prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS as soon as call sites showed stable argument types, improving header usefulness for adjacent decompilation.
Inserted named VsModeData members into gmm_x0 at offsets that are directly consumed by newly matched functions, reducing opaque padding while preserving the struct size boundary.
Used #pragma dont_inline around gm_801B1F70, suggesting a targeted matching tactic where inlining behavior affected code generation.
Consolidated includes in gm_1B03.c by using gm_unsplit.h instead of a list of several gm_1601/gm_16AE/gm_16F1/gm_18A5/gm_1A3F/gm_1A45/gm_1A4C/gm_1BFA headers.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling game-mode setup code, first recover the StartMeleeData, GameRules, PlayerInitData, and VsModeData relationships; many functions become thin wrappers or field initializers once those types are available.
A block of seemingly unrelated minor-scene functions may be a repeated template over different gmMainLib_804D3EE0 VsModeData offsets, with only the data pointer and small constants changing.
Callback argument signatures can often be inferred from helper call sites such as gm_801A583C/gm_801A5C3C; once inferred, raw offset writes should be replaced with typed PlayerInitData fields.
Replacing padding with named struct fields should be driven by exact offset use in matched functions and should preserve known following fields and total struct size.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: changing UNK_PARAMS to concrete pointer types in one module may require including the right shared type header in another module.
Signedness matters for small table accessors: gm_801BF648 was corrected from u8 to s8, likely because its backing field and semantic use are signed.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names were retained for unknown game-mode routines, e.g. gm_801B1F70, gm_801B69C0, gm_801BF4DC.
Lifecycle suffixes OnInit and OnLoad were preserved for scene lifecycle hooks such as gm_801B6808_OnLoad and gm_801B8D60_OnInit.
Callback-like local functions used fn_ prefixes with address names, e.g. fn_801B1F6C, fn_801B8C5C, fn_801B8E50, and fn_801B9060.
Recovered struct members remain offset-derived unk names when semantics are not proven, e.g. gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_D10, unk_6D0, unk_E50, unk_F90, unk_10D0, unk_1490, and unk_1710.
Unused parameters are explicitly named unused in callback signatures when required for ABI/signature matching but not read.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_801B1F70 to control compiler output for matching.
Used explicit unsigned constants in wrappers, e.g. gm_801A5AF0(scene, 4U, 3U), where literal type likely mattered for codegen.
Used exact float literals such as 0.35f, 0.5f, 1.0f, 1.5f, and 1.8f in PlayerInitData and rules setup.
Used an explicit cast in temp_r31->rules.xE = (u16) gm_801BF694(), documenting the target field width and likely matching generated truncation.

Retained an apparently unused local variable in gm_801B1F70, PlayerInitData* var_r30 = &data->players[i], which may have been needed for register allocation or matching even though it is not used later in the shown diff.
Converted M2C_FIELD(arg0, s16*, 0x12) to arg0->x12 after recovering PlayerInitData*, improving readability without losing the offset behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No PR body text was present in the provided context; rationale is inferred from the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_1490 or unk_1710 to semantic mode names.
Search for gm_801A583C and gm_801A5C3C call patterns to recover more PlayerInitData callback signatures.
Find subsequent work around gm_801B91C8, gm_801B927C, gm_801B931C, and other remaining UNK_RET functions adjacent to this matched block.
Check whether gm_801BF648 signedness affected other callers or table definitions for gm_8049E548.pad_0.
Search for additional StartMeleeData rule initializer functions using gm_80167A64, gm_80167A14, and gm_8016F088.
